mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, which drives expected corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or the Rank IC of PAYEMS changes versus 20-day returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, labor market slack keeps the Federal Reserve on hold with accommodative policy, lowering the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations, or the mean forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a synchronized recovery in supply and labor markets, boosting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of INDPRO growth and UNRATE declines fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a regression versus individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero lower bound monetary regime, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) compress the ex-ante real policy rate, reducing the real cost of capital and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the Rank IC is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal strengthening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the information coefficient is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production (INDPRO) growth exceeding 0.5% indicates accelerating manufacturing output that supports earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5% is followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the mean excess return over cash is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 0.25%) and accelerating nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a goldilocks recovery where growth is improving without threatening near-term tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signals of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and PAYEMS growth fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction (F-test p > 0.05) beyond main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales (RSAFS) declines below zero trigger earnings downgrade expectations and negative consumer-spending sentiment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 45%.
